title: Hamnet
logline: When Agnes, a woman with mystical gifts, marries the restless young Will Shakespeare, their love produces three children—including a son named Hamnet—but when plague strikes their family eleven years later, grief and distance threaten to destroy their bond until Will transforms their loss into his greatest work.
antagonistOrConflict: Death, grief, and the unbridgeable distance between a husband and wife processing loss in fundamentally different ways
tone: Lyrical, operatic, intimate, dreamlike—grounded in visceral domesticity yet elevated by mythic imagery of nature and mortality
themes: Grief and the transformation of loss into art
The circular nature of life and death
The tension between domestic life and artistic ambition
The power and cost of creation

actBreaks.description: Agnes discovers she's pregnant with twins, marking the end of courtship and the beginning of family life that will define the central tragedy.

validation.ruling: Scene #45 (p38, 31.9%) ends Act 1 with Will's violent outburst when he hurls his writing materials at the wall in manic frustration, revealing his possession by creative demons and inability to function in domestic life. This irreversible moment fundamentally shifts the story's direction, forcing Agnes to recognize Will must leave for London, and the story cannot return to their earlier romantic harmony.

actBreaks.description: Will leaves for London after Hamnet's death, the marriage fracturing as grief drives them apart and the forest enters winter—the lowest point before transformation.

validation.ruling: Scene #87 (p78, 65.5%) ends Act 2 with Agnes and Mary washing and preparing Hamnet's body for burial, with Mary revealing she knew Agnes's mother and bringing herbs to tuck into the burial sheet. This irreversible death and the unexpected connection between Agnes and Mary marks the story's darkest point and forces Agnes into the final act's journey toward confronting Will about their loss.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script operates as a grief spiral that ultimately completes itself. It opens with death (the opera, the red egg, the ancient tree) and closes by returning to the same forest, now reborn. The structure mirrors the circular tree roots from the opening image—life feeding death feeding life. Rather than a traditional three-act rise, this is a triptych: courtship and conception (scenes 1-45), family life and loss (scenes 46-87), and separation leading to artistic resurrection (scenes 88-125). The twin births at scene 45 are the hinge that makes Hamnet's death inevitable; Will's departure at scene 87 is the nadir that makes the Globe performance necessary.

The script refuses linear causality. Time jumps freely—'30 years ago,' '17 years ago,' '11 years later'—treating memory and present as equally real. Scenes 73-74 intercut Hamnet's death with Will riding home and a dreamlike Globe Theatre that doesn't yet exist, collapsing past/present/future into a single eternal moment. This is a script about how grief destroys time, and how art reconstructs it.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The first act (scenes 1-45) moves in sensual, elongated beats—long dialogue scenes in the forest (scene 21 runs 5 pages), the apple shed seduction (scene 28), the marriage negotiation (scene 34). The pacing is patient, almost hypnotic. Act two accelerates: Hamnet's illness and death (scenes 66-84) compress into rapid intercutting between the dying child and Will's oblivious journey. The final act slows again into grief's suspended animation—months pass in single scenes (87, 93, 100) until the Globe performance (scenes 119-124) stretches a single play into 15 pages of operatic catharsis. The script breathes: expansion, contraction, release.

incitingIncident.description: Will hurries through the garden toward Agnes, they lock eyes and stare at each other with intense pull, exchange brief words about his tutoring, and she asks what he wants before he requests to walk with her.

validation.ruling: Scene #5 (p4, 3.4%) is the inciting incident where Will and Agnes meet for the first time in the garden, experiencing an intense, magnetic pull toward each other that neither can resist. This encounter shatters Will's ordinary world as a tutor and establishes the central romantic conflict that drives the entire narrative forward.

midpoint.description: Agnes finds Hamnet alone and crying in the garden; she comforts him and reads his palm, telling him he will grow up to work with his father in the London theatre.

validation.ruling: Scene #66 is too passive and comforting for a midpoint. Scene #64 (p60, 50%) is the true midpoint where Will describes his vision of their family living together in London while Agnes warns him autumn brings illness to children, and he insists he must return to finish his work. This is the point of no return where Will commits to his proactive choice to pursue his London ambitions despite Agnes's warnings, raising the stakes significantly as his decision will have fatal consequences for Hamnet.

climax.description: Will removes his Ghost costume in the side wing and breaks down sobbing in excruciating pain as the hard shell he built around himself shatters completely.
climax.sceneHeading: I/E. GLOBE THEATRE - DAY [BREAK DOWN SCENE]

validation.ruling: Scene #120 is setup, not climax—Agnes is still watching the play unfold. Scene #121 (p113, 95%) is the true climax where Will removes his Ghost costume and breaks down sobbing in excruciating pain as the hard shell he built around himself shatters completely. This is the peak emotional confrontation where Will finally faces the full weight of his grief and guilt, resolving the central question of whether he can process Hamnet's death and demonstrating his character arc's completion.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - DAY
scenes.summary: A red egg rests among tree roots in an ancient British forest while Purcell's opera merges with the soundscape of the canopy.

scenes.heading: INT. HEWLANDS - STUDY - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Will, a Latin tutor with ink-stained nails and a cut above his eyebrow, stands agitated at a window while three farm boys recite Latin phrases behind him.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - ANCIENT TREE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Agnes sleeps restlessly among the roots of an ancient tree, then wakes when her hawk appears and prepares to receive it on her gloved hand.

scenes.heading: INT. HEWLANDS - STUDY - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Will turns from the reciting boys to see Agnes walking through the field with her hawk, and dread washes over him as he realizes he must stay trapped in this room.

scenes.heading: EXT. HE WLANDS - GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Will hurries through the garden toward Agnes, they lock eyes and stare at each other with intense pull, exchange brief words about his tutoring, and she asks what he wants before he requests to walk with her.
scenes.turningPointNote: First meeting between Will and Agnes — the central relationship of the story begins

scenes.heading: INT. HEWLANDS - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes enters the pristine kitchen where her stepmother Joan brushes her daughter's hair, and Joan questions where Agnes has been and criticizes her disheveled appearance.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER AVON - EVENING
scenes.summary: Will paces along the riverbank, then walks into the river for a swim and dives underwater.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRATFORD - EVENING
scenes.summary: Will walks into Stratford-upon-Avon during evening chores, passing locals, carts, animals, and timber-framed houses.

scenes.heading: EXT. HENLEY STREET - EVENING
scenes.summary: Will walks to his home on the corner of Henley Street.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - PASSAGEWAY - EVENING
scenes.summary: Will enters the dark passageway of his home filled with glover's tools and the murmur of prayer, transforming into a different creature as he drags his feet toward the dining room.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Will sits down to a late supper with his family, fights with Gilbert over bread, and answers his mother's questions about tutoring at Hewlands while his father John remains silent.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: When the humans sleep, unseen presences wake in the house — residue by the fireplace, whispering tools, and stains on the wall.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - PASSAGEWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Something moves through the dark passageway, a deeper shade of black like a void or tunnel into the unknown.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Will lies asleep on a wool bale in the attic surrounded by candle stubs, ink, and papers where he writes, mumbling and dreaming with his hand pressed to his forehead.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - ANCIENT TREE - DAY 30 YEARS AGO
scenes.summary: Thirty years ago, the red egg rests among the roots of the ancient tree in the same place Agnes will later sleep.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - DAY 30 YEARS AGO
scenes.summary: A wood-dweller named Rowan collects herbs in the forest while humming, then sees a sheep-farmer whistling at his dog in the distant field below.

scenes.heading: EXT. EDGE OF THE FOREST - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard the sheep-farmer sees Rowan emerging from the forest edge, waves shyly, and she steps through the ferns onto his cultivated land out of curiosity.

scenes.heading: EXT. EDGE OF THE FOREST - DAY PRESENT DAY
scenes.summary: Will stands in the field where Richard once stood and heads into the forest.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - LIGHT PATH - DAY
scenes.summary: Will follows a path through tall plants, sees Agnes's hawk gliding overhead, then closes his eyes to listen to the forest's repetitive rhythm.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - DARK PATH - DAY
scenes.summary: Will finds Agnes foraging and watches her gather herbs, smell twigs, and taste flowers, then shouts to stop her from receiving her hawk on her bare arm, startling her.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - ANCIENT TREE - DAY
scenes.summary: Will and Agnes arrive at the ancient tree where he confesses he wishes to see her, she challenges him to tell her a story that moves him, and he recounts Ovid's tale of Iphis and Ianthe while she listens intently.
scenes.turningPointNote: Will reveals himself through storytelling, deepening their connection and establishing the foundation of their relationship

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - LIGHT PATH - LATER
scenes.summary: Agnes puts on Will's glove and extends her arm to receive her hawk, which lands gracefully, and Will touches the hawk's feathers as it looks at him.

scenes.heading: INT. HEWLANDS - STUDY - DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Will stands by the window waiting for Agnes while the boys recite Latin, then sees her walking through the garden with milk pails and she stops to look up at him.

scenes.heading: EXT. HEWLANDS - GARDEN - SAME
scenes.summary: Agnes sees Will looking small and trapped behind the metal window and wants to free him but must go.

scenes.heading: INT. HEWLANDS - STUDY - SAME
scenes.summary: Will watches Agnes leave, leans against the window framed by metal edges, and feels the heat burning in his stomach as he realizes something has to change.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - WORKSHOP - DAY WEEKS LATER
scenes.summary: Will turns over pairs of gloves in the workshop, watches his drunk father John criticize Edmond's stitching, then suddenly stands and heads toward the door despite John's protest.

scenes.heading: EXT. HEWLANDS - GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Will finds Agnes in the garden and declares he must be hand-fasted to her, that no one else would do, and he will speak to her family regardless of their objections.
scenes.turningPointNote: Will proposes hand-fasting to Agnes, initiating the marriage that will define the rest of the story

scenes.heading: INT. HEWLANDS - APPLE SHED - DAY
scenes.summary: Will and Agnes are crushed together in the dark apple shed where she undoes his jerkin, he asks if she's certain, and they begin to touch each other with nervous intensity.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - DAY
scenes.summary: The forest exists in an erotic pause of summer, with nature continuously having sex with everything around it to create new life.

scenes.heading: INT. HEWLANDS - KITCHEN - DAY 17 YEARS AGO
scenes.summary: Seventeen years ago, heavily pregnant Rowan grinds herbs at her wild kitchen table while teaching young Agnes and Bartholomew the Nine Herb Charm to treat Bartholomew's cut hand.

scenes.heading: INT. HEWLANDS - KITCHEN - MORNING PRESENT DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes sits at the same table in the now-clean kitchen, lost in memories, then puts her hand on her belly realizing she's pregnant and looks up at her stepmother Joan.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER AVON - DAY
scenes.summary: Will swims against the river current, dives underwater tumbling through the light-filled world, then emerges gasping and floats with his body carried by the water.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - D INING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes waits in the Henley house dining room while Mary holds her rosaries in horror, then Will enters surprised to find Agnes there and Mary reveals Agnes is pregnant with his child and has been banished.
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes arrives pregnant at Will's family home, forcing the marriage and changing both their lives irrevocably

scenes.heading: EXT. HENLEY HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Bartholomew walks out with Agnes following, she asks him to care for her hawk, he questions why she would choose Will's family and this place, and they walk in silence as he struggles with tears knowing everything will change.

scenes.heading: INT. CHURCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A rushed secret wedding is held at night in the church with both families present, and Agnes grows uneasy among the dark stone arches, chanting the Nine Herb Charm while overwhelmed by dread.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY STREET - ATTIC - MORNING 7 MONTHS LATER
scenes.summary: Seven months later, heavily pregnant Agnes wakes in the marriage bed to find Will having a nightmare beside her, rises with difficulty, walks to the window murmuring about dense branches, and presses her hand to her belly.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - MORNING (FOR LATER, SAME LIGHTING
scenes.summary: Will picks up a paper with broken slanted letters reading "branches cannot feel rain" and something hits him as he realizes its meaning.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - PASSAGEWAY - DAWN MONTHS LATER
scenes.summary: Heavily pregnant Agnes walks through the dark passageway carrying a basket, stops to hold back contraction pain with her hand against her mouth, knowing she must not scream and must get to the forest.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - LIGHT PATH - MORNING
scenes.summary: Agnes walks toward the heart of the forest and leans against a tree to rest when pain begins traveling from her lower back deep between her legs.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - ANCIENT TREE - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes lowers herself to the ground at the ancient tree, crouches through relentless waves of pain gripping soil and moss, and worries as her body proves inadequate against the agony.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - ANCIENT TREE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Will and Bartholomew find Agnes dozing with a newborn baby girl at her breast, Bartholomew cuts the umbilical cord and carries Agnes while Will holds the baby and notices the blood on the ground and a large hole under the ancient tree.
scenes.turningPointNote: Susanna is born — the first child and first major life event in Will and Agnes's marriage

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER AVON. - DAY A YEAR LATER
scenes.summary: A year later, Will stands on the riverbank lost in thought.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - WORKSHOP - DAY A WEEK LATER
scenes.summary: John's rough hands pour love into making an intricate pair of gloves while Will watches briefly then returns to his own meaningless work that drives him mad.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes plays with one-year-old Susanna in the kitchen while doing chores with Mary, then smells something strange like food gone off and sniffs around trying to locate the source.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Agnes wakes from a dream realizing she's pregnant again, sees Will hunched over his desk cutting feathers and drinking, calls him to bed but he stays manic insisting he needs to work, and she tries unsuccessfully to connect with him.

scenes.heading: EXT. HEWLANDS - FIELD - MORNING
scenes.summary: Agnes sits on a fence with Bartholomew and confesses Will can't sleep, smells sour like mold, is sick with himself, and needs to go to London, while Bartholomew protests and questions what's in London.
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes decides Will must go to London, setting in motion the separation that will define their marriage and grief

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - KITCHEN - MORNING MONTHS LATER
scenes.summary: Visibly pregnant Agnes wraps food for Will's journey to London while sitting still and hiding her feelings, as Will paces anxiously excited about leaving and talks about the dangerous river currents.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER AVON - DAY MONTHS LATER
scenes.summary: A storm breaks the riverbank and water rushes onto land.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - DAY
scenes.summary: Very pregnant Agnes is alone on the attic floor trying to find a comfortable position when she realizes it's time and knows she needs to get to the forest.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes hurries to the kitchen to gather her things when a wave of pain hits, Mary enters and refuses to let her go to the forest, and Gilbert helps restrain Agnes as she struggles and begs to be released.

scenes.heading: EXT. HENLEY STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Floodwater mixed with filth and rubbish flows down the middle of Henley Street.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - PASSAGEWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Water creeps under the door and floods the passageway floor.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes labors in the flooded dining room while Eliza sweeps water out; Agnes becomes delirious, calling for her absent husband and speaking incoherently about gloves.

scenes.heading: INT. HEWLANDS - DINING ROOM - NIGHT 17 YEARS AGO
scenes.summary: Young Agnes witnesses her mother Rowan lying dead in childbirth with a stillborn baby in her arms while village women and a priest perform last rites.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM -PRESENT DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes gives birth to a lifeless baby girl; the midwife slaps the baby but it does not cry, and Agnes demands to hold the child despite warnings.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - MORNING TIME PASSING
scenes.summary: The camera observes Agnes's postpartum body in detail, showing stretch marks, scars, and physical changes from childbirth.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - MORNING 11 YEARS LATER
scenes.summary: Eleven-year-old twins Judith and Hamnet secretly swap clothes and disguise themselves as each other by the attic window.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The twins run through the dining room and Mary scolds them to conduct themselves properly.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: The twins rush into the kitchen and pretend to peel apples while still disguised; Will arrives home from London and watches his family, and the twins excitedly greet him.

scenes.heading: EXT. HENLEY HOUSE - GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Will surprises Agnes in the garden where their three children perform lines from his writing dressed as witches.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - ANCIENT TREE - THE NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: The family buries Agnes's dead hawk in the forest; Agnes places Will's glove in the grave and they plant lily of the valley bulbs over it.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - LIGHT PATH - DAY
scenes.summary: The family walks through the forest singing together while Agnes braids flower crowns and the children play.

scenes.heading: EXT. EDGE OF FOREST - DAY
scenes.summary: Will grows wary while watching his family play; Hamnet notices and Will warns him to stay away from his grandfather and reveals he's leaving for London tomorrow.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Will describes to Agnes his vision of their family living together in London while she warns him that autumn brings illness to children; he insists he must go back to finish his work.

scenes.heading: EXT. HENLEY HOUSE - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Will says goodbye to his children and watches them walk away until they disappear, then stands frozen before finally leaving.

scenes.heading: EXT. HENLEY HOUSE - GARDEN - LATER
scenes.summary: Agnes finds Hamnet alone and crying in the garden; she comforts him and reads his palm, telling him he will grow up to work with his father in the London theatre.
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes makes a prophetic promise to Hamnet about his future that will become tragically ironic after his death.

scenes.heading: EXT. LONDON - RIVER THAMES - AFTERNOON TWO WEEKS LATER
scenes.summary: Will eats a pie by the Thames near plague refugee tents, then throws the rest to a homeless dog and walks away.

scenes.heading: EXT. LONDON - CHARTERHOUSE - ROAD - EVENING
scenes.summary: Will walks through London trying to turn street noise into rhythm when he sees watchmen in plague masks boarding up a house while people inside wail.

scenes.heading: EXT. LONDON - CHARTERHOUSE - COURTYARD - EVENING
scenes.summary: Will arrives at his courtyard apartment where travelers have set up camp; he watches a shadow puppet show performed by a masked puppet master.

scenes.heading: EXT. HEWLANDS - GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: A swarm of bees masses in the orchard at Hewlands; Agnes tells Bartholomew something has upset them and to keep the children inside, then feels suddenly restless.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - DAY
scenes.summary: Hamnet finds Judith asleep holding his baby blanket; when she wakes, he notices she is warm and sees a swelling at the base of her chin.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hamnet discovers Judith has plague symptoms, setting the tragedy in motion.

scenes.heading: EXT. HENLEY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes arrives home through misty rain.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Hamnet tells Agnes that Judith is ill; Agnes drops everything and rushes toward the attic.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - D AY
scenes.summary: Agnes finds Judith with buboes and realizes she has the plague; she sends Hamnet to fetch Mary immediately.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: The family works to keep Judith warm by the fire while Agnes feeds her medicine; Judith convulses and Hamnet holds her hand.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Agnes refuses to leave Judith's side through the night, insisting she will not let her daughter die; Mary prays quietly.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary tells Eliza to write to Will; Susanna asks if Judith will die and Mary's silence confirms it may be tonight.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - DAWN
scenes.summary: Hamnet wakes at dawn feeling wrong and remembers Judith is sick; he crawls from bed in pain to find his sister.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - DAWN
scenes.summary: Hamnet finds Judith looking at him with strange silver eyes as she is dying; he crawls to her and their fingers twine together as her eyes roll back.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - DAWN
scenes.summary: Will rides desperately toward home at dawn, then stops in a field and looks up to see a hawk circling in the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. GLOBE THEATRE - FOREVER DAWN
scenes.summary: Hamnet finds himself in a dreamlike version of the Globe Theatre before it was built, walking through fog until he sees a lone tree and yearns for his mother.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - DAWN
scenes.summary: Agnes wakes and finds both children on the pallet; Judith's fever has lowered, but when Agnes turns Hamnet over, he is pale and dying with plague symptoms.
scenes.turningPointNote: The devastating reversal: Judith survives but Hamnet has contracted the plague and is dying.

scenes.heading: INT. GLOBE THEATRE - FOREVER DAWN
scenes.summary: Hamnet lies down under the tree in the dreamlike Globe, telling himself he will be brave and rest just for a moment.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Hamnet's body convulses violently as he dies; Agnes desperately tries remedies while Judith recovers across the room, but nothing works and Agnes panics.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Village women pressure Mary to let them prepare Hamnet's body for burial according to plague law; Agnes is curved over Hamnet's body before he is wrapped.

scenes.heading: EXT. HENLEY HOUSE - BACKYARD - MORNING
scenes.summary: Bartholomew burns the bedding and mattress in the backyard, then gently places Hamnet's baby blanket into the fire.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Agnes washes Hamnet's body with Mary's help, wrapping him in a lavender-scented sheet with herbs; Mary brings her the items and helps her prepare their son.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - DAWN A DAY LATER
scenes.summary: Agnes lies sleepless while Will paces; she blames herself for not seeing Hamnet was sick, and Will tries to comfort her but she cannot be consoled.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - GARDEN - DAY THREE WEEKS LATER
scenes.summary: Will finds Judith crying in the garden holding Hamnet's sword; Susanna angrily tells her to stop crying, then breaks down herself before rushing off to do chores.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - PASSAGEWAY - EVENING
scenes.summary: Will encounters John in the passageway who cannot find words of comfort; Will sees Mary crying by the fire and stands frozen in the passageway, unable to move forward or back.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - KITCHEN - MORNING NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Will tells Agnes he must leave for London; she is shocked and insists he cannot leave, but he says the theatres are reopening and he must go.
scenes.turningPointNote: Will announces he is abandoning his grieving family to return to London, fracturing his marriage.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FOREST - TIME PASSES
scenes.summary: The winter forest is covered in death and dead leaves bury the red egg.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAVEYARD - DAY
scenes.summary: Lily of the valley blooms on Hamnet's small grave in soft rain.

scenes.heading: EXT. HENLEY HOUSE - BACKYARD - MORNING MONTHS LATER
scenes.summary: Agnes's garden is grey and barren in winter.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTI C - MORNING
scenes.summary: Agnes sits at Hamnet's desk where his belongings remain untouched while Will's are gone; she hovers her hand over his arranged stones, trying to understand their meaning.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Susanna reads Will's sonnet aloud; Judith asks if their father stays away because she reminds him of Hamnet, and Susanna insists he will return.

scenes.heading: EXT. HENLEY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: After midnight, Agnes wanders the empty street calling out for Hamnet and listening for a response.

scenes.heading: EXT. LONDON - CHARTERHOUSE - COURTYARD - DAY
scenes.summary: Will paces in the courtyard directing actors in a Hamlet scene; he grows increasingly frustrated and angry with their delivery, like a cornered wild animal.

scenes.heading: EXT. LONDON - RIVER THAMES - DOCK - EVENING
scenes.summary: Will wanders onto an empty dock at night running lines; he stares at the still dark water and is suddenly filled with dread and hopelessness, wanting to disappear.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW HOUSE - MONTHS LATER
scenes.summary: Agnes stands before a massive new house holding a set of keys, looking confused and consumed by grief; Bartholomew watches from a distance as she stares at the cold keys.
scenes.turningPointNote: Will has bought Agnes a grand estate in his absence, a gesture that underscores the emotional chasm between them.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - SAME EVENING
scenes.summary: Will distributes gifts at a celebratory dinner while telling a humorous story, but Agnes watches him silently as the room's noise blurs around her.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Agnes works on her herb book in the attic when Will offers her a shawl; she confronts him about avoiding her, refusing to move because Hamnet died in the house, and accuses him of infidelity, causing Will to break down sobbing.
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes directly confronts Will about his avoidance, Hamnet's death, and his infidelity — the first open acknowledgment of the unbridgeable distance between them.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - ATTIC - NIGHT A MONTH LATER
scenes.summary: The attic stands completely empty the night before the family moves to the new house.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY H OUSE - PASSAGEWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Agnes walks slowly through the passageway, looking into each room and listening.

scenes.heading: INT. HENLEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Agnes stands at the dining room door where Hamnet was born and died, observing that nothing has changed except that he is not there.

scenes.heading: EXT. LONDON - GLOBE THEATRE - TIME PASS ES
scenes.summary: Construction shots show the Globe Theatre being built from natural materials.

scenes.heading: I/E. LONDON - GLOBE THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Will stands alone on the incomplete Globe stage staring at a mysterious void painted in the backdrop, no longer afraid but hardened.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW HOUSE - GARDEN - MORNING SIX MONTHS LATER
scenes.summary: Six months later, Agnes's garden blooms with apple trees, bees, lavender, and Judith's cats with kittens, while Agnes continues gathering herbs in wet, filthy skirts despite living in the new fancy house.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW HOUSE - WALKWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Susanna hurries through a walkway wearing the house keys at her waist, her face tense.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW HOUSE - GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Joan visits Agnes in the garden to check on Will after John's death; Agnes claims Will writes often, but Susanna's look suggests otherwise.

scenes.heading: I/E. GLOBE THEATRE - MORNING THREE DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Wind whistles through the Globe's wooden beams as a hooded hawk sits on a post beside a lone tree on the empty stage.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE LONDON - MORNING
scenes.summary: London appears as a broken, cluttered city with the river winding through it and smoke rising.

scenes.heading: EXT. LONDON - CHARTERHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes and Bartholomew navigate the noisy, stench-filled London streets past dead bodies on poles, with Agnes focused on finding her husband for information.

scenes.heading: EXT. LONDON - CHARTERHOUSE - COURTYARD - DAY
scenes.summary: A boy directs Agnes and Bartholomew to Will's attic room through the dark archway.

scenes.heading: INT. LONDON - WILL’S ATTIC - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes discovers Will's sparse attic room with a low bed and writing table filled with handwritten pages, confused about why the owner of Stratford's largest house lives here.
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes discovers the stark reality of Will's London life — a revelation that deepens her confusion about who her husband has become.

scenes.heading: INT. GLOBE THEATRE - DRESSING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Will closes his eyes in the dressing room, listening to the players and crowd, then ritualistically applies white makeup to his face and beard.

scenes.heading: EXT. GLOBE THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes and Bartholomew approach the towering Globe Theatre and see playbills everywhere bearing her son's name.
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes sees Hamnet's name on playbills at the Globe — the first indication that Will has used their son's death in his work.

scenes.heading: I/E. GLOBE THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes and Bartholomew push through hundreds of shouting audience members in the circular pit, passing vendors and nursing mothers, as more people pour through the doors.

scenes.heading: I/E. GLOBE THEATRE - DAY [FIRST SCENE]
scenes.summary: The play begins with actors speaking about a ghost; Agnes asks Bartholomew what this has to do with her boy as Bernardo describes seeing his father's ghost in armor.

scenes.heading: I/E. GLOBE THEATRE - DAY [GHOST SCENE]
scenes.summary: Agnes watches the Fool with a hawk mock Claudius onstage; when she tries to leave, a young man dressed and painted to look like Hamnet enters as Hamlet, and Will appears as the Ghost calling Hamlet his son, causing Agnes to collapse.
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes witnesses Will performing as the Ghost calling the Hamnet-like actor 'my son' — the devastating revelation that Will has transformed their dead child into public spectacle, causing her complete collapse.

scenes.heading: I/E. GLOBE THEATRE - DAY [BREAK DOWN SCENE]
scenes.summary: Will removes his Ghost costume in the side wing and breaks down sobbing in excruciating pain as the hard shell he built around himself shatters completely.

scenes.heading: I/E. GLOBE THEATRE - DAY [TO BE OR NOT TO BE]
scenes.summary: The play continues silently as the perspective shifts to Hamnet's ghostly viewpoint, floating from the backdrop's void through the stage to watch Agnes's tear-stained face and Will performing, unable to touch either parent.

scenes.heading: I/E. GLOBE THEATRE - DAY [OPHELIA SCENE]
scenes.summary: Ophelia grieves under a rowan tree with herbs and flowers, humming a tune; Agnes recognizes herself in Ophelia's wild grief while Will watches Agnes from the curtains.

scenes.heading: I/E. GLOBE THEATRE - DAY [FINAL SCENE]
scenes.summary: Hamlet and Laertes fence fiercely as Agnes watches with tears and a smile, remembering Hamnet's dream; Gertrude drinks poison and collapses, Laertes and Hamlet wound each other with the poisoned sword, and all die as Agnes weeps and the audience calls for a happy ending.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - TIMELESS
scenes.summary: The forest comes alive in spring as a crack appears on the red egg's shell, signaling rebirth.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final image of the cracked egg and spring rebirth offers symbolic closure and the possibility of renewal after devastating loss.
